title: Korean Gochujang Crispy Chicken
description: High-protein crispy air fryer chicken tossed in a sweet-spicy Korean gochujang sauce. Marinated, cornstarch-coated, and air fried for maximum crunch with minimal fat.
ingredients: 1700g (60oz) chicken breast, cut into bite-sized pieces
instructions: Cut 1700g chicken breast into bite-sized pieces and place in a large bowl.
Add 4 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp garlic salt, 1 tbsp black pepper, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, and 2 beaten eggs to the chicken. Mix thoroughly to coat. Cover and marinate in the fridge for at least 2 hours.

While chicken marinates, make the sauce: combine 0.5 cup low-sodium soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 2 tbsp gochujang paste, 2 tbsp maple syrup, 1 tbsp minced garlic, and 1 tbsp minced ginger in a small saucepan. Simmer over medium heat for 3–4 minutes until slightly thickened. Set aside.
Remove chicken from marinade. Drain excess marinade and pat pieces dry with paper towel — this is critical for crust formation.
Toss dried chicken pieces in 30g cornstarch immediately before air frying. Do not do this step ahead of time.
Preheat air fryer to 400°F. Working in single-layer batches, air fry chicken for 10–12 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crispy. Do not crowd the basket.
Once all batches are done, toss hot chicken in the gochujang sauce and serve immediately.
notes: Sauce goes on after air frying — never before or you lose the crisp. Low-sodium soy sauce is strongly recommended; standard soy sauce brings this to ~1,100mg sodium per serving. Maple syrup replaces honey here with no macro difference. Brown sugar from the original has been removed — the gochujang and maple syrup provide enough sweetness. Pairs well with steamed rice and edamame or a kale side.
